ai_features_disclosed.description: Gradient-boosted decision-tree ensemble plus a neural feature extractor produce a binary credit decision (approve / decline) and a numeric risk score for personal loan applications between $5,000 and $50,000. The vendor recommends autonomous deployment for applications below $25,000; the deploying organization may set its own threshold.

When the model declines an application, it produces up to four ranked reason codes (e.g., 'insufficient credit history', 'high debt-to-income ratio') intended to satisfy adverse-action notice requirements under ECOA and Reg B.

training_data_sources: Vendor's historical credit-decisioning labeled dataset (3 million applications, 2018-2024)
Public credit bureau aggregated statistics (TransUnion, Equifax)

categories: Bank account balance and transaction history (with consumer consent)
pii_processing_claims.handling_notes: Vendor retains decision records for 7 years per Reg B requirements. PII is encrypted at rest and in transit. The vendor states model fairness was audited against ECOA-protected classes in 2024; the audit report is available under NDA on request.

data_residency.conditions: All processing in US data centers. No cross-border transfers. Vendor does not currently support deployments outside US jurisdiction; for Canadian deployments, model would need to be re-trained on Canadian credit bureau data and re-audited.
